title.zh: 量子真空中的「雙人舞」：科學家首次捕捉到夸克自旋糾纏的瞬間
title.en: The Quantum Tango: Scientists Capture First Evidence of Entangled Quark Spins in the Vacuum

summary.zh:
在布魯克海文國家實驗室的相對論性重離子對撞機（RHIC）中，STAR實驗團隊發現了一個驚人現象：當高能質子對撞時，會從量子真空中釋放出成對的奇夸克與反奇夸克，它們的自旋狀態竟保持著高度相關性。這些虛粒子對在形成Λ超子後，仍保留著(18±4)%的相對極化信號，就像一對跳著默契雙人舞的舞者。

這項發表在《自然》期刊的研究，首次將量子色動力學（QCD）中「手徵對稱性自發破缺」的抽象概念轉化為可測量的實驗證據。當超子對的角度分離增大時，這種相關性會消失——這正是量子系統退相干的特徵。研究團隊透過分析數百萬次質子對撞事件，為夸克禁閉與量子糾纏的動態關係提供了全新見解，可能解開『為什麼基本粒子具有質量』這個深奧謎題的關鍵線索。
summary.en:
In a groundbreaking experiment at Brookhaven's Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC), the STAR collaboration has observed spin correlations between strange quarks and antiquarks emerging from the quantum vacuum during high-energy proton collisions. These ephemeral particle pairs, which later coalesce into Λ hyperons, retain a measurable (18±4)% relative polarization—a telltale signature of their quantum entanglement at the moment of creation.

The study, published in Nature, bridges the gap between theoretical predictions of chiral symmetry breaking in quantum chromodynamics (QCD) and experimental observation. The team found that the spin correlation vanishes when hyperon pairs are widely separated, demonstrating the expected decoherence of the quantum system. By analyzing millions of proton-proton collisions, the researchers have established a new experimental paradigm for studying how quark confinement dynamically interacts with quantum entanglement—potentially shedding light on the deep connection between mass generation and the structure of the QCD vacuum.

keyArticle.title: Measuring spin correlation between quarks during QCD confinement
